article.title: Supersonik | Run client demos 24/7 with AI

text:
Five months ago I joined Supersonik. Honestly, I had no idea what I was getting myself into… I didn’t know how the startup world worked, or if I would fit in. But those doubts quickly disappeared.
One of my goals has always been to surround myself with the best people  and when I met  Daniel Carmona Serrat, Pol Ruiz Farre, and Joaquim Lecha I knew this was one of those once-in-a-lifetime opportunities.
Week by week, it became clear that what we were building wasn’t “normal.” Like with any revolution, there are plenty of unknowns along the way… but being part of it is just too much fun. Why do I call it a revolution? 
Because I deeply believe Supersonik is going to change the way we Sell.
 Imagine an AI Agent capable of selling any software product, in any language, 24/7, simultaneously.
Sounds pretty revolutionary, right? 
👉 If you want to try it out, here’s the link:

author.occupation: GTM - Founding Team @ Supersonik

text:
The wait is over. Today we’re launching Supersonik, the first AI that can run a live demo of your product.

Proud to share that we’ve also raised $5M led by Andreessen Horowitz with participation from angels at Google, DeepMind, Salesforce & more.

A few months ago, Pol Ruiz Farre, Joaquim Lecha and I set out to rethink how products get sold: no waiting, no drop-offs, just instant, always-on demos. After thousands of live calls, seeing Supersonik actually deliver value has been surreal.

This journey has taught us so much already. To all the founders out there: we are living in the fastest moving times in history, everything is possible. Whether you are from Barcelona, like we are, or from any other place in the world, it has never been easier to build globally and have an impact. Dream it, build it, ship it. Please reach out to me if I can ever help you with anything.

If you’re curious about what we’ve built at Supersonik, we’d love for you to experience it firsthand. Join an instant demo linked in the comments below 👇

Finally, here's a quick launch video that captures everything in 50 seconds :)
